# Wicklee — Full LLM Reference

> Sovereign GPU fleet monitor for local AI inference.
> This document contains everything an AI agent needs to integrate with Wicklee.

---

## Architecture

Wicklee consists of three components:
1. **Agent** — Single Rust binary per node. Runs as a system service (launchd/systemd).
   Embeds a React dashboard at localhost:7700. Harvests hardware telemetry at 1 Hz.
2. **Cloud Backend** — Rust/Axum on Railway. Receives telemetry from agents (2s push),
   stores in Postgres, serves fleet dashboard via SSE at wicklee.dev.
3. **Frontend** — React 19 + Vite + Tailwind. Dark mode. Two builds: agent-embedded
   (localhost) and cloud-deployed (wicklee.dev).

## Install

```bash
# macOS / Linux
curl -fsSL https://wicklee.dev/install.sh | bash

# Windows (PowerShell as Administrator)
irm https://wicklee.dev/install.ps1 | iex

# Register as system service (auto-starts on boot, runs as root)
sudo wicklee --install-service
```

---

## Localhost API

Base URL: `http://localhost:7700`
Auth: None required. All endpoints available on every tier.

### GET /api/metrics
SSE stream. 1 Hz cadence. Returns `data: {MetricsPayload}\n\n` per tick.

### GET /ws
WebSocket. 1 Hz cadence. Same MetricsPayload as SSE. Fallback transport — browser tries WebSocket first, falls back to SSE if unavailable.

### GET /api/observations
Returns 17 server-side observation patterns evaluated by the Rust agent against a 10-minute DuckDB buffer every 10 seconds. Patterns are pushed to the fleet cloud when paired.
```json
{ "observations": [{ "pattern_id": "thermal_drain", "severity": "warning", ... }] }
```

### GET /api/profile?minutes=60
Inference Profiler — correlated timeline of TTFT, tok/s, KV cache %, queue depth, thermal penalty, power. Resolution auto-scales by time range. Max 1440 minutes (24h).
```json
{ "node_id": "WK-XXXX", "range_minutes": 60, "resolution_s": 10, "samples": [{ "ts_ms": 1234, "model": "llama3.1:70b", "tok_s": 42.5, "ttft_ms": 145, "kv_cache_pct": 87, "queue_depth": 2, "power_w": 12.1, "thermal_state": "Normal", "thermal_penalty": 1.0, "gpu_util_pct": 85 }] }
```

### GET /api/cost-by-model?hours=24&kwh_rate=0.12
Cost attribution per model — daily power cost breakdown by model. Max 720 hours (30 days).
```json
{ "node_id": "WK-XXXX", "range_hours": 24, "kwh_rate": 0.12, "models": [{ "model": "llama3.1:70b", "hours_active": 6.2, "avg_watts": 12.3, "cost_usd": 0.0092, "tok_s_avg": 42.5 }], "total_cost_usd": 0.0211 }
```

### GET /api/explain-slowdown?ts_ms=1234567890
Root cause analysis for a slow inference request. Finds closest trace, reads ±30s hardware context, evaluates 6 factors, generates natural-language summary.
```json
{ "request": { "ts_ms": 1234, "model": "llama3.1:70b", "ttft_ms": 2100, "latency_ms": 8500 }, "factors": [{ "factor": "kv_cache_pressure", "severity": "high", "detail": "KV cache at 93%", "value": 93.2 }], "summary": "TTFT spiked to 2.1s primarily due to KV cache at 93% (high) and thermal penalty at 1.75x." }
```

### GET /api/model-comparison?hours=168&kwh_rate=0.12
Side-by-side efficiency data for every model that has run on this node. Answers "which model is most efficient on my hardware?" with real measured data.
```json
{ "node_id": "WK-XXXX", "range_hours": 168, "models": [{ "model": "llama3.1:70b-q4", "hours_active": 18.3, "avg_tok_s": 22.5, "avg_watts": 12.1, "wes": 1.86, "avg_ttft_ms": 245, "cost_per_hour": 0.0015, "total_cost": 0.027 }, { "model": "phi3:mini", "hours_active": 42.0, "avg_tok_s": 85.1, "avg_watts": 3.1, "wes": 27.5, "avg_ttft_ms": 34, "cost_per_hour": 0.0004, "total_cost": 0.017 }] }
```

### GET /api/history?node_id=WK-XXXX
DuckDB metric history. 1-hour of raw samples.
```json
{ "samples": [{ "ts_ms": 1774621630223, "tok_s": 19.5, "gpu_util_pct": 45.0, "gpu_power_w": 2.1, "mem_pressure_pct": 52.0, "swap_write_mb_s": 0.0 }] }
```

### GET /api/traces
Proxy inference traces (when proxy is enabled).
```json
[{ "id": "uuid", "ts_ms": 1774621630223, "model": "llama3.1:8b", "latency_ms": 650, "ttft_ms": 45, "tpot_ms": 32.5, "eval_count": 20 }]
```

### GET /api/events/history
Node event log from DuckDB.
```json
{ "events": [{ "ts_ms": 1774621630223, "event_type": "startup", "level": "info", "message": "Agent started v0.7.10", "node_id": "WK-XXXX" }] }
```

### GET /api/events/recent
Recent in-memory events (last 20, 5-minute TTL).

### GET /api/export?format=json|csv
Full data export. Supports `format=json` or `format=csv`.

### GET /api/tags
Ollama model tags (proxied from local Ollama instance).
```json
{ "models": [{ "name": "llama3.1:8b", "size": 5368709120 }] }
```

### GET /api/pair/status
Pairing status with fleet.
```json
{ "status": "connected", "node_id": "WK-XXXX", "fleet_url": "https://wicklee.dev" }
```

### POST /api/insights/dismiss
Permanently dismiss a local observation pattern.

### GET /api/insights/dismissed
List all permanently dismissed pattern IDs.

---

## MCP Server (Model Context Protocol)

The agent exposes a JSON-RPC 2.0 MCP endpoint for AI agents. Available on all tiers, localhost only, no auth required.

### POST /mcp
JSON-RPC 2.0 endpoint. Content-Type: application/json.

Tools:
- `get_node_status` — Full MetricsPayload snapshot (~60 fields: CPU, GPU, memory, power, thermal, inference state, model, WES penalty, tok/s)
- `get_inference_state` — Current inference state (live/idle-spd/busy/idle) with which detection tier matched and sensor values
- `get_active_models` — List of loaded models across Ollama (model, size, quantization, tok/s), vLLM (model, requests, cache), llama.cpp (model, tok/s, slots)
- `get_observations` — 17 patterns with per-observation routing_hint (steer_away/reduce_batch/monitor) + node-level routing_hint + routing_hint_source
- `get_metrics_history` — 1-hour rolling telemetry (redirects to REST /api/history)

Resources:
- `wicklee://node/metrics` — Live MetricsPayload JSON
- `wicklee://node/thermal` — Thermal state, penalty_avg, penalty_peak, thermal_source, sample_count

Example request:
```json
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"get_node_status"},"id":1}
```

### GET /.well-known/mcp.json
MCP server discovery manifest. Returns server name, version, transport, and capabilities.

---

## Fleet API v1

Base URL: `https://wicklee.dev/api/v1`
Auth: `X-API-Key: wk_live_...` header. Create keys in Settings > API Keys.

### GET /api/v1/fleet
All nodes with full MetricsPayload.
```json
{
  "nodes": [{
    "node_id": "WK-XXXX",
    "hostname": "macmini.local",
    "online": true,
    "last_seen_ms": 1774622475195,
    "metrics": { ...full MetricsPayload... }
  }]
}
```

### GET /api/v1/fleet/wes
WES scores for all nodes, ranked.
```json
{
  "nodes": [
    { "node_id": "WK-XXXX", "online": true, "wes": 15.0 },
    { "node_id": "WK-99E9", "online": true, "wes": 3.2 }
  ]
}
```

### GET /api/v1/nodes/{node_id}
Single node deep dive with full MetricsPayload.
```json
{
  "node_id": "WK-XXXX",
  "hostname": "macmini.local",
  "online": true,
  "last_seen_ms": 1774622475195,
  "metrics": { ...full MetricsPayload... }
}
```

### GET /api/v1/route/best
Routing recommendation. Returns two candidates: latency (highest tok/s) and efficiency (highest WES).
```json
{
  "latency":    { "node": "WK-99E9", "tok_s": 31.9, "wes": 3.3,  "reason": "Highest throughput" },
  "efficiency": { "node": "WK-XXXX", "tok_s": 19.5, "wes": 15.0, "reason": "Highest WES" },
  "default":    "efficiency"
}
```

### GET /api/v1/insights/latest
Fleet intelligence snapshot. Team tier and above only.
```json
{
  "generated_at_ms": 1774624251478,
  "fleet": { "online_count": 3, "total_count": 3, "avg_wes": 9.9, "fleet_tok_s": 79.0 },
  "findings": [{ "node_id": "WK-99E9", "hostname": "spark-c559", "severity": "low", "pattern": "wes_below_baseline", "title": "WES below fleet average", "detail": "WES 3.3 vs fleet average 9.9", "value": 3.3, "unit": "WES" }]
}
```

### POST /api/v1/keys
Create a new API key. Returns the raw key once (prefix `wk_live_`). Stored as SHA-256 hash.
```json
{ "key": "wk_live_abc123...", "key_id": "uuid", "created_at_ms": 1774622475195 }
```

### GET /api/v1/keys
List all API keys. Hash is never exposed.

### DELETE /api/v1/keys/{key_id}
Revoke an API key. Takes effect immediately.

---

## MetricsPayload Schema

The full telemetry payload sent every 1-2 seconds. All fields are optional (null when unavailable).

### Hardware
- `node_id`: string — Stable WK-XXXX identifier
- `hostname`: string — System hostname
- `gpu_name`: string — "Apple M4", "NVIDIA RTX 4090", etc.
- `cpu_usage_percent`: f32
- `total_memory_mb`: u32
- `used_memory_mb`: u32
- `available_memory_mb`: u32
- `cpu_core_count`: u32
- `memory_pressure_percent`: f32 — macOS memory pressure (0-100)
- `swap_write_mb_s`: f32 — Swap write rate in MB/s

### Power
- `cpu_power_w`: f32 — CPU package power (RAPL on Linux, IOKit on macOS)
- `apple_soc_power_w`: f32 — Combined CPU+GPU+ANE (Apple Silicon only)
- `apple_gpu_power_w`: f32 — GPU-only power (Apple Silicon)
- `nvidia_power_draw_w`: f32 — Board power (NVIDIA)

### GPU
- `gpu_utilization_percent`: f32 — Apple Silicon GPU residency
- `nvidia_gpu_utilization_percent`: f32 — NVIDIA GPU utilization
- `nvidia_vram_used_mb`: u32
- `nvidia_vram_total_mb`: u32
- `nvidia_gpu_temp_c`: f32
- `gpu_wired_limit_mb`: u32 — Apple Silicon VRAM budget

### Thermal
- `thermal_state`: string — "Normal" | "Fair" | "Serious" | "Critical"
- `thermal_source`: string — "nvml" | "iokit" | "coretemp" | "clock_ratio" | "sysfs" | "wmi"
- `penalty_avg`: f32 — Rolling average thermal penalty (1.0 = no penalty)
- `penalty_peak`: f32 — Peak penalty in sample window
- `clock_throttle_pct`: f32 — Clock speed reduction percentage

### Inference State
- `inference_state`: string — "live" | "idle-spd" | "busy" | "idle"
- `ollama_running`: bool
- `ollama_active_model`: string — "llama3.1:8b"
- `ollama_model_size_gb`: f32
- `ollama_quantization`: string — "Q4_K_M"
- `ollama_tokens_per_second`: f32 — From 20-token probe or proxy
- `ollama_inference_active`: bool
- `vllm_running`: bool
- `vllm_model_name`: string
- `vllm_tokens_per_sec`: f32

### Latency & Queue (v0.7.10+)
- `ollama_ttft_ms`: f32 — Time to first token (probe baseline, ~30s)
- `ollama_prompt_eval_tps`: f32 — Prompt processing speed (tok/s)
- `ollama_load_duration_ms`: f32 — Model load time
- `vllm_avg_ttft_ms`: f32 — TTFT from Prometheus histogram (production)
- `vllm_avg_e2e_latency_ms`: f32 — End-to-end latency (production)
- `vllm_avg_queue_time_ms`: f32 — Queue wait time (production)
- `vllm_requests_waiting`: u32 — Queue depth
- `vllm_requests_swapped`: u32 — Swapped requests
- `vllm_total_prompt_tokens`: u64 — Cumulative prompt tokens
- `vllm_total_generation_tokens`: u64 — Cumulative generation tokens
- `ollama_proxy_avg_ttft_ms`: f32 — TTFT from proxy done packets
- `ollama_proxy_avg_latency_ms`: f32 — E2E latency from proxy
- `ollama_proxy_request_count`: u64 — Total proxied requests

### WES
- `wes_version`: u32 — Currently 2
- `sample_count`: u32 — Samples in current WES window

### Agent
- `agent_version`: string — "0.7.10"
- `timestamp_ms`: u64 — Unix milliseconds

---

## WES — Wicklee Efficiency Score

```
WES = tok/s / (Watts x ThermalPenalty)
```

When thermal is Normal (penalty = 1.0): WES = tok/s / Watts = tok/W.

### Thermal Penalties
| State    | Penalty | Score Multiplier |
|----------|---------|-----------------|
| Normal   | 1.00    | 1.00x           |
| Fair     | 1.25    | 0.80x           |
| Serious  | 1.75    | 0.57x           |
| Critical | 2.00    | 0.50x           |

### Color Scale (WES and tok/W)
| Range | Label      | Color        |
|-------|------------|-------------|
| > 10  | Excellent  | Emerald     |
| 3-10  | Good       | Light Green |
| 1-3   | Acceptable | Yellow      |
| < 1   | Low        | Red         |

---

## Inference State Machine

Four states, evaluated once per second as a pure function:

1. **Tier 1 (Exact):** vLLM requests_running > 0 OR llama.cpp slots_processing > 0 → LIVE
2. **Tier 2 (Attribution):** Ollama /api/ps expires_at change attributed to user (not probe) within 15s → LIVE
3. **Tier 3 (Physics):** GPU residency > 20%, SoC power > 8W, ANE power > 0.5W, or NVIDIA power > 40W → LIVE
4. **Default:** No signal → IDLE (no model) or IDLE-SPD (model loaded, baseline tok/s from probe)

---

## 18 Observation Patterns + 5 Fleet Alerts

### Agent-Evaluated (17 patterns)
Evaluated by the Rust agent against a 10-minute DuckDB buffer every 10 seconds.
Pushed to the fleet cloud when paired for fleet-wide visibility.

Community (9):
- **thermal_drain:** Sustained throttle reducing tok/s > 8% vs Normal baseline
- **phantom_load:** Model loaded + power > 5W + tok/s < 0.5 (idle model burning power)
- **wes_velocity_drop:** WES declining > 10% before thermal state changes
- **memory_trajectory:** Memory pressure rising > 1 pct/min sustained
- **power_jitter:** Power CV > 20% during inference
- **swap_io_pressure:** Swap write > 2 MB/s sustained (model spilling to disk)
- **clock_drift:** Clock throttle > 15% with Normal thermal
- **nvidia_thermal_redline:** GPU temp > 85C sustained or > 90C instant
- **vram_overcommit:** Model size > 90% of VRAM capacity

Pro (8):
- **power_gpu_decoupling:** High power + low GPU% during inference
- **bandwidth_saturation:** Low GPU% + high VRAM + dropping WES
- **efficiency_drag:** WES penalty > 30% with Normal thermal
- **pcie_lane_degradation:** PCIe link width below rated max (NVIDIA only)
- **vllm_kv_cache_saturation:** KV cache > 90% sustained
- **ttft_regression:** TTFT slope > +5 ms/min with mean > 100 ms
- **latency_spike:** Recent E2E latency > 1.5x baseline and > 500 ms
- **vllm_queue_saturation:** requests_waiting avg > 3 sustained

### Cloud-Evaluated (1 pattern)
- **fleet_load_imbalance:** Node WES > 20% below best healthy peer (Pro)

### Fleet Alerts (5, all tiers)
Evaluated by the cloud backend every 60 seconds.
- **zombied_engine:** Inference stuck in "busy" > 10 minutes
- **thermal_redline:** Node in "Critical" thermal for 2+ consecutive ticks
- **oom_warning:** Memory pressure > 95% for 2+ consecutive ticks
- **wes_cliff:** WES < 35% of 24h baseline during active inference
- **agent_version_mismatch:** Agent version differs from fleet majority

---

## Sampling Cadence

| Subsystem | Cadence | What |
|-----------|---------|------|
| Broadcast loop | 1 Hz | Assembles snapshot, pushes to SSE/WS |
| System metrics | 1 Hz | CPU, memory, swap via sysinfo |
| Apple Silicon power | 5s window | powermetrics (requires root) |
| NVIDIA GPU | 1 Hz | NVML API (zero-privilege) |
| Ollama harvester | 2s | GET /api/ps (models, VRAM, expires_at) |
| Ollama probe | ~30s | 20-token POST /api/generate (disabled when proxy active) |
| vLLM harvester | 2s | GET /metrics (Prometheus gauges + histograms) |
| WES thermal sampler | 2s | 30-sample rolling window |
| DuckDB local store | 2s | Batch INSERT, WAL mode |
| Cloud telemetry push | 2s | POST to fleet URL (~2 KB per push) |

---

## Sovereignty

- Inference content (prompts, responses, conversations) NEVER leaves the machine
- Only hardware telemetry (CPU%, GPU%, power, thermal, model name) is transmitted when paired
- Agent runs as a system service with no external dependencies
- All data visible in the Observability tab's Sovereignty section
- Cloud relay is optional — localhost dashboard works fully offline

---

## Pricing

| Tier | Price | Nodes | History | Patterns |
|------|-------|-------|---------|----------|
| Community | Free | 3 | 24h | 9 |
| Pro | $9/mo | 10 | 7 days | 18 |
| Team | $19/seat/mo | 25 | 90 days | 18 + shared dashboards |
| Enterprise | From $200/mo | Custom | Custom | + proxy + sovereign deploy |

---

## Runtimes

| Runtime | Platforms | Detection |
|---------|-----------|-----------|
| Ollama | macOS, Linux, Windows | Process scan + /api/ps |
| vLLM | Linux | Process scan + /metrics Prometheus |
| llama.cpp / llama-box | macOS, Linux | Process scan + /health |
